Name: Recognize, commend, and support the MTA’s planned display of the American flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge in commemoration of the 250th anniversary of the United States.
Title: Resolution to recognize, commend, and support the Metropolitan Transportation Authority’s planned display of the American flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge in commemoration of the 250th anniversary of the United States in 2026 and to encourage similar commemorative initiatives associated with the Semiquincentennial.
BodyName: Committee on Cultural Affairs, Libraries and International Relations

History.Description: This Resolution was Introduced by Council
This Resolution was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Cultural Affairs, Libraries and International Relations
History.AgendaNote: Cultural Affairs, Libraries and International Relations

Text:
Whereas, The year 2026 will mark the 250th anniversary of the founding of the United States, known as the Semiquincentennial, a historic milestone of national significance; and
      Whereas, The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA") owns and operates the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, one of the most iconic and heavily traveled bridges in the United States; and
      Whereas, The Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge serves as a symbolic gateway to Staten Island and Brooklyn and is viewed by millions of residents, commuters, and visitors each year; and
      Whereas, To commemorate the nation's Semiquincentennial, the MTA plans to install an American flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge in connection with the nation's 250th anniversary celebration; and
      Whereas, According to local newspapers in Staten Island and Brooklyn, Staten Island resident, Alesio Bejleri, brought early attention to the idea of flying an American flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge to commemorate America's Semiquincentennial, and enlisted the assistance of the borough leaders; and  
      Whereas, Such an installation will provide a powerful visual tribute to the nation's founding principles of liberty, unity, and resilience; and 
      Whereas, Staten Island is home to one of the highest concentrations of veterans in New York City and maintains a strong tradition of patriotic observances and civic pride; and 
      Whereas, Similar flag displays at prominent infrastructure sites across the United States have served as meaningful civic landmarks and sources of community pride; and 
      Whereas, There is demonstrated bipartisan support among elected officials representing Staten Island for the installation of a flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge; now, therefore, be it
      Resolved, That the Council of the City of New York recognizes, commends, and supports the Metropolitan Transportation Authority's planned display of the American flag on the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge in commemoration of the 250th anniversary of the United States in 2026 and encourages similar commemorative initiatives associated with the Semiquincentennial. 
       
      
JPB
LS # 23987
7/2/2026 1:00 PM
2
